content:
Summary: Wired the Personal Records screens to the new Supabase-backed backend. Added a dedicated backend PHR module with typed read/create endpoints for BMI, blood pressure, temperature, and medication intake, then updated the Flutter BMI, blood pressure, temperature, and medicine screens to load and save through the API using the authenticated access token. Verified with backend build success and frontend flutter analyze success.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] If needed later, add richer PHR DTOs or read models for BMI age/gender metadata, which are still UI-only and not persisted in the current schema.
2. [ongoing] Consider adding dedicated edit/delete flows for the new PHR endpoints if product requirements expand beyond create/read.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\app.module.ts — Registered the new PersonalRecordsModule so the backend exposes the PHR routes. | Notes: Imports now include the dedicated PHR module.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\auth\auth.module.ts — Exported AuthSupportService so the new PHR module can reuse authenticated user resolution. | Notes: Keeps auth boundary shared without duplicating token parsing.
3. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\personal-records.module.ts — Introduced a dedicated module for PHR persistence and controllers. | Notes: Owns the new domain boundary.
4. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\personal-records.controller.ts — Added typed /phr endpoints for list/create operations across all four record types. | Notes: Controller returns explicit promise types.
5. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\personal-records.service.ts — Added orchestration between auth resolution and repository mapping for PHR operations. | Notes: Handles per-user access and request normalization.
6. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\personal-records.repository.ts — Added typed Supabase read/write access for bmi_records, blood_pressure_records, temperature_records, and medication_intake_records. | Notes: Includes typed row mapping and insert helper.
7. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\personal-records.types.ts — Defined backend response/input contracts for the new PHR endpoints. | Notes: Keeps API shapes explicit.
8. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\dto\create-bmi-record.dto.ts — Validated BMI create payloads at the API boundary. | Notes: Uses class-validator with numeric coercion.
9. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\dto\create-blood-pressure-record.dto.ts — Validated blood pressure create payloads at the API boundary. | Notes: Covers systolic/diastolic and optional fields.
10. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\dto\create-temperature-record.dto.ts — Validated temperature create payloads at the API boundary. | Notes: Supports celsius and fahrenheit inputs.
11. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\personal-records\dto\create-medication-intake-record.dto.ts — Validated medication intake create payloads at the API boundary. | Notes: Includes ISO timestamps and status enum validation.
12. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features\phr\data\personal_records_api_client.dart — Added a typed HTTP client for the new PHR backend endpoints. | Notes: Includes response parsers for all four record types.
13. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features\phr\body_mass_index\presentation\screen\body_mass_index_screen.dart — Switched BMI history and create flow from local state to backend persistence. | Notes: Keeps the UI composition but loads/saves via the API.
14. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features\phr\body_mass_index\presentation\models\body_mass_index_models.dart — Added mapping from backend BMI responses to the UI history model. | Notes: Preserves UI formatting while sourcing data from Supabase.
15. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features\phr\blood_pressure\presentation\screen\blood_pressure_screen.dart — Switched blood pressure history and create flow from local state to backend persistence. | Notes: Added backend loading, save flow, and error states.

content:
Summary: Implemented the first Personal Records persistence slice with separate Supabase tables for BMI, blood pressure, temperature, and medicine intake. Added a new SQL migration, updated typed database contracts, and aligned the schema documentation and backend README with the new tables. Verified the backend still builds successfully after the type updates.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Wire the frontend Personal Records screens to these new backend tables through a typed API layer.
2. [ongoing] Add backend repositories/controllers for create and read flows once the API contract is finalized.
3. [ongoing] Consider a shared observations/read-model layer later if dashboard or timeline views need combined rollups.

Affected files:
1. [created] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\sqls\006_personal_records_setup.sql — Defines the new persistent database schema for personal records with separate tables, RLS, and indexes. | Notes: Includes BMI, blood pressure, temperature, and medication intake tables.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\supabase\database.types.ts — Adds strongly typed Supabase table contracts for the new personal record tables so backend data access remains type-safe. | Notes: Includes explicit row, insert, and update shapes for each table.
3. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\data-structure.md — Keeps the architecture document aligned with the implemented personal records schema and the new table-per-screen approach. | Notes: Updated the Personal Records section and BMI notes.
4. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\README.md — Documents that the new personal records bootstrap script should be run alongside the auth/profile bootstrap script. | Notes: Helps keep setup instructions consistent with the new migration.

content:
Summary: Changed the profile personal information screen from a card-based layout to a flatter BMI-like page structure after the user clarified they wanted no card styling. The screen now uses a plain header, inline profile summary row, divider separation, and flat form sections while keeping the existing save/reset behavior intact.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Apply the same flat, non-card visual direction to nearby profile screens if they still use heavy card shells and need visual unification.

Affected files:
1. [modified] frontend/lib/features/profile/presentation/screens/personal_information_screen.dart — Removed the card-like shells and replaced them with a flat header, summary row, divider, and form section. | Notes: Kept the behavior and compatibility parameters unchanged.
2. [modified] frontend/test/features/profile/presentation/screens/personal_information_screen_test.dart — Updated the test description to reflect the flat layout while preserving the main content assertions. | Notes: No functional assertions were removed.

content:
Summary: Fixed the Security Settings screen crash caused by calling ScaffoldMessenger.of(context) during initState-driven async hydration. The login redirect is now deferred with a post-frame callback, which keeps the cached preloaded Security screen behavior intact and avoids inherited-widget timing errors. Flutter analysis passed after the fix.

Tasks:
1. [finished] Preserve the instant-loaded Security screen experience while preventing inherited-widget access during initState-driven async paths.
2. [finished] Verify the deferred redirect fix with a fresh Flutter analysis run.
3. [finished] Record the crash fix and cache behavior in project memory for future retrieval.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features\profile\presentation\screens\security_settings_screen.dart — Changed the login redirect to run after the first frame so the screen no longer throws when initState-triggered async work calls ScaffoldMessenger too early. | Notes: The rest of the cache-first loading behavior remains intact.

content:
Summary: Added a plain-English atlas for WAH4P so future work can read the project like a map. The repo is best understood in layers: a Flutter client that controls navigation and presentation, a NestJS backend that owns security and business rules, and Supabase/Postgres plus secure local storage for persistence. The most important user journeys now have an explicit flow: splash/startup -> onboarding or login -> dashboard or MPIN unlock -> feature areas like records, appointments, resupply, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. This memory also clarifies which screens are static, which are route-driven, and which backend APIs support each major journey.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Maintain this atlas-style memory alongside future feature changes so the project map stays easy to read for navigation, auth, and data work.
2. [ongoing] Reconcile older route documentation with the live Flutter route tree when updating screen inventories or onboarding future contributors.
3. [ongoing] Use the layer model in future work plans: start from frontend flow, then check backend API support, then verify data persistence rules.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\wah4p_app.dart — The app shell controls theme, startup gating, and resume-time locking, making it the top-level navigation anchor for the project map. | Notes: Central entry point for the Flutter client.
2. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_startup_service.dart — Defines the route decision logic for onboarding, login, dashboard, and MPIN unlock, which is essential to the launch map. | Notes: Startup flow source of truth.
3. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_routes.dart — Lists the canonical route names used throughout the Flutter app. | Notes: Best place to understand route coverage.
4. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_router.dart — Maps route names to screens and shows transition style choices for each user flow. | Notes: Best source for the screen-to-route atlas.
5. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\startup_gate_screen.dart — Shows the transient gate screen that hands off to the resolved initial route during startup. | Notes: Launch UX and routing handoff.
6. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_lock_state_service.dart — Implements the time-based unlock policy used when the app resumes from background. | Notes: Important for security and session behavior.
7. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features — The feature directory structure reveals the major product areas and how the client is partitioned by domain. | Notes: Useful for understanding the app map by feature group.
8. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\app.module.ts — Shows the backend’s top-level composition, including global guards and core modules. | Notes: Server architecture anchor.

11. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\auth\profile.controller.ts — Provides authenticated profile read/update endpoints separate from the workflow controller. | Notes: Profile API map.

14. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\checklist.md — Tracks unfinished persistence and validation work across the product and shows which areas still need implementation. | Notes: Project readiness map.

The repo is best understood in layers: a Flutter client that controls navigation and presentation, a NestJS backend that owns security and business rules, and Supabase/Postgres plus secure local storage for persistence. The most important user journeys now have an explicit flow: splash/startup -> onboarding or login -> dashboard or MPIN unlock -> feature areas like records, appointments, resupply, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. content:
Summary: Saved a deeper project map for WAH4P that focuses on navigation, feature boundaries, and service responsibilities. The repository presents a clear two-tier architecture: a NestJS backend that owns auth/security/business rules and a Flutter frontend that owns display, navigation, and device-scoped state. The frontend route tree now has a precise map from startup through onboarding, auth, dashboard, PHR, EHR-style records, appointments, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. The backend API surface is also clearer: a public health check plus a broad auth/profile domain that covers registration, login, 2FA, MPIN, password reset, and profile hydration/update. This memory layer is intended to function as an app map, not just a summary.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Reconcile SCREENS.md and any other route docs with the live frontend/lib/app/app_routes.dart and frontend/lib/app/app_router.dart map.
2. [ongoing] Keep capturing future changes as route-group or workflow-group memory updates so the app map remains easy to follow as features grow.
3. [ongoing] Use the startup/auth/profile structure already identified here when planning future changes, especially anything involving route gating, MPIN, login, or profile updates.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\wah4p_app.dart — Defines the Flutter app shell, theme, route entrypoint, and resume-time lock behavior, which are central to the app map. | Notes: Confirms where app-wide navigation and lifecycle control live.
2. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_router.dart — Contains the full named route registry and screen-to-route mapping for the Flutter client. | Notes: This is the best source of truth for the app’s visible navigation tree.
3. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_routes.dart — Lists every canonical route constant used by the Flutter app. | Notes: Useful for maintaining route names and screen grouping.
4. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\startup_gate_screen.dart — Shows how the app waits for startup resolution and then redirects to the correct initial route. | Notes: Important for understanding the launch flow.
5. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_startup_service.dart — Implements startup routing decisions from auth, onboarding, and MPIN state. | Notes: This is the decision engine behind splash-to-app routing.
6. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\app\app_lock_state_service.dart — Implements the app’s background timing and unlock requirement policy. | Notes: Key to understanding resume/lock behavior.
7. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\frontend\lib\features — Directory structure reveals the major presentation feature families that make up the client. | Notes: Confirms the app is organized by domain feature.
8. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\app.module.ts — Defines backend module composition and global guards, which frame the server-side architecture map. | Notes: Shows the core backend wiring.
9. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\auth\auth.module.ts — Shows how the backend auth domain is composed from controllers, services, and repositories. | Notes: Critical for understanding auth responsibility split.
10. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\auth\auth.controller.ts — Lists the auth workflow endpoints and groups them into registration, login, 2FA, security, and MPIN routes. | Notes: Primary backend workflow surface.
11. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\auth\profile.controller.ts — Exposes profile hydration and update endpoints separate from the auth workflow controller. | Notes: Important for profile screen integration.
12. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\src\health\health.controller.ts — Provides the public health endpoint and marks the small public server surface. | Notes: Useful for operational checks.

The frontend route tree now has a precise map from startup through onboarding, auth, dashboard, PHR, EHR-style records, appointments, calendar, notifications, profile, and legal screens. content:
Summary: Audited the WAH4P repository and saved a clear project snapshot for future work. The repo is split into a NestJS backend, a Flutter frontend, and supporting SQL/docs. The backend is a secured API with global API-key protection, throttling, strict env validation, Supabase auth/data integration, and Resend email delivery. The frontend is a Flutter mobile app with centralized theming, secure local storage, a startup gate, and MPIN/session lifecycle handling. The docs describe a Supabase/Postgres source of truth for patient data, with frontend screens mostly acting as presentation and interaction layers. I also captured the current testing surface and a couple of documentation mismatches that future work should reconcile.

Tasks:
1. [ongoing] Reconcile SCREENS.md and related docs with the current frontend tree so the route inventory matches the live Flutter codebase.
2. [ongoing] Continue enforcing the backend env contract so every required secret and origin value is provided in .env.example and validated at startup.
3. [ongoing] Use the existing backend and frontend test scaffolding as the baseline for future changes, and expand tests where new screen or auth flows are added.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\AGENTS.md — Contains repository-wide operating rules, architecture reminders, and documentation guidance that shape how the project should be understood. | Notes: Established the backend/frontend responsibility split and project conventions.
2. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\data-structure.md — Defines the canonical data model, storage layers, and screen-to-data mapping used by the app. | Notes: Important for understanding persistence and source-of-truth rules.
3. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\checklist.md — Lists the current persistence and validation work items across the app and acts as a project readiness tracker. | Notes: Useful for identifying unfinished foundation work.
4. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\SCREENS.md — Documents the screen inventory and route mapping, which is critical for understanding the frontend surface area. | Notes: Contains a likely path mismatch with the live repo layout.
5. [inspected] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\wah4patients\backend\package.json — Shows the backend stack, scripts, test tooling, and key runtime dependencies. | Notes: Confirms NestJS, Supabase, Resend, Jest, and validation tooling.

metadata.summary: Audited the WAH4P repository and saved a clear project snapshot for future work. The repo is split into a NestJS backend, a Flutter frontend, and supporting SQL/docs. The backend is a secured API with global API-key protection, throttling, strict env validation, Supabase auth/data integration, and Resend email delivery. The frontend is a Flutter mobile app with centralized theming, secure local storage, a startup gate, and MPIN/session lifecycle handling. The docs describe a Supabase/Postgres source of truth for patient data, with frontend screens mostly acting as presentation and interaction layers. I also captured the current testing surface and a couple of documentation mismatches that future work should reconcile.
